mission.title: Install and Configure ClickHouse
mission.description: ClickHouse is a real-time analytics database management system designed for generating analytical data reports quickly and efficiently. It is ideal for applications requiring high-speed data processing and real-time analytics. Key trade-offs include its column-oriented storage, which optimizes read performance but may require more complex data modeling.

steps.title: Add the ClickHouse Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official ClickHouse Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add clickhouse https://github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse

```

> This is the official repository for ClickHouse.

steps.title: Install ClickHouse CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions required for ClickHouse to function properly in Kubernetes.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse/master/deploy/kubernetes/crds/clickhouse.altinity.com_clickhouses.yaml

```

> This CRD is necessary for managing ClickHouse clusters.

steps.title: Install ClickHouse using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs ClickHouse in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install clickhouse clickhouse/clickhouse --namespace clickhouse --create-namespace --version 26.2.4.23-stable

```

> Replace 'clickhouse' with your desired release name.

steps.title: Create a ClickHouse Cluster Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a custom resource that defines your ClickHouse cluster configuration.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: clickhouse.altinity.com/v1
kind: ClickHouseCluster
metadata:
  name: my-clickhouse
spec:
  configuration:
    clusters:
      - name: my-cluster
        layout:
          shardsCount: 1
          replicasCount: 1
EOF

```

> This creates a simple ClickHouse cluster with one shard and one replica.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: clickhouse.altinity.com/v1
kind: ClickHouseCluster
metadata:
  name: my-clickhouse
spec:
  configuration:
    clusters:
      - name: my-cluster
        layout:
          shardsCount: 1
          replicasCount: 1
EOF

steps.title: Verify ClickHouse Cluster Status
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the ClickHouse cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get clickhousecluster -n clickhouse

```

> You should see your ClickHouse cluster listed with a status of 'Running'.

uninstall.title: Uninstall ClickHouse
uninstall.description:
This step removes the ClickHouse installation and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall clickhouse --namespace clickhouse

kubectl delete namespace clickhouse

```

> This command will remove the ClickHouse release.
> This will delete the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade ClickHouse
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This step will upgrade ClickHouse to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl cp my-clickhouse-0:/var/lib/clickhouse/data /path/to/backup

helm upgrade clickhouse clickhouse/clickhouse --namespace clickhouse --version 26.2.4.23-stable

kubectl get clickhousecluster -n clickhouse

```

> Backup your ClickHouse data.
> This command upgrades ClickHouse to the specified version.
> Verify that the ClickHouse cluster is running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to ClickHouse server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The ClickHouse server may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the ClickHouse pods with 'kubectl get pods -n clickhouse' and ensure they are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No nodes available for ClickHouse'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be insufficient resources allocated for the ClickHouse pods.

**Fix:**
Ensure your Kubernetes cluster has enough resources (minimum 2 vCPU, 4GB RAM per node).

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ClickHouse cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The ClickHouse cluster may not have completed its initialization.

**Fix:**
Wait for a few minutes and check the status again with 'kubectl get clickhousecluster -n clickhouse'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create ClickHouse database'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The ClickHouse user may not have the necessary permissions.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the user has the correct permissions to create databases in ClickHouse.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new SQL functions and improved performance for large datasets.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old configuration format for clusters.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from version 25.x, ensure to migrate your configuration to the new format.

resolution.summary: You should see the ClickHouse cluster listed with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get clickhousecluster -n clickhouse'.

EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources and the required tools installed.
